name: Castillo de Gómez Arias — circular desde Benamejí
region_intro: Jogging routes around Benamejí offer a diverse landscape, characterized by the meandering Genil River and its banks, extensive olive groves, and the adapted Vía Verde de la Subbética. The region's terrain includes low-difficulty paths along the river, as well as more varied routes through pine and olive groves. Features like "La Grieta" and Cerro del Viento provide elevated viewpoints and natural surroundings for runners. Best jogging routes around Benamejí The most popular jogging route is From Encinas Reales to Río Anzur — circular, a 4.8 miles (7.7 km) trail that takes about 1 hour to complete, following the Genil River through pine and olive groves. Another top favourite among local runners is Castillo de Gómez Arias — circular desde Benamejí, a moderate 5.5 miles (8.9 km) path offering views of the historic castle and surrounding landscapes. Local runners also love the Laguna de los Jarales — circular by the Protection Zone Natural Reserve Laguna de los Jarales, a 7.6 miles (12.2 km) trail leading through the natural reserve, often completed in about 1 hour 20 minutes. Jogging around Benamejí is defined by the Genil River, vast olive groves, and the Vía Verde de la Subbética. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes catering to various ability levels. The routes in Benamejí are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 7 reviews. More than 40 runners have used komoot to explore Benamejí's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Benamejí?
A: Benamejí offers a good selection of running routes, with over 20 trails to explore. These routes cater to various fitness levels, including easy, moderate, and more challenging options.

Q: Are there easy running routes suitable for beginners in Benamejí?
A: Yes, Benamejí has easy running routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed jog. Many paths along the Genil River banks offer low-difficulty terrain, suitable for all the family. While most routes are moderate, there is at least one specifically rated as easy.

Q: Can I find long-distance running trails around Benamejí?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking longer distances, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1287223600/from-encinas-reales-to-benameji-circular-by-the-camino-de-los-olivares" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Encinas Reales to Benamejí — circular by the Camino de los Olivares&lt;/a&gt; offer a significant challenge, covering over 15 kilometers through varied terrain, including extensive olive groves.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect on Benamejí's running trails?
A: The running trails in Benamejí showcase a diverse landscape. You'll encounter the meandering Genil River and its banks, vast olive groves characteristic of the region, and sections of the Vía Verde de la Subbética. Many routes also pass through pine groves, orchards, and riparian forests, offering picturesque and varied scenery.

Q: Are there any circular jogging routes in Benamejí?
A: Yes, many of Benamejí's jogging routes are circular, providing convenient starting and ending points. Popular options include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1287172524/from-encinas-reales-to-rio-anzur-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Encinas Reales to Río Anzur — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which follows the Genil River, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1287208757/castillo-de-gomez-arias-circular-desde-benameji" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Gómez Arias — circular desde Benamejí&lt;/a&gt;, offering views of the historic castle.

Q: What interesting sights or landmarks can I see while jogging in Benamejí?
A: While jogging in Benamejí, you can enjoy views of several interesting landmarks and natural features. Routes often pass by the historic Arias Gómez Castle, the Renaissance bridge of Hernán Ruiz II, and ancient Arab-origin water wheels along the Genil River. You might also encounter viewpoints like 'La Grieta' or 'Cerro del Viento' offering panoramic vistas. Specific highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5392067" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Genil River Meander&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5392069" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Our Lady of Fuensanta&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are the running routes in Benamejí suitable for families?
A: Many of the routes along the Genil River are low-difficulty and suitable for families, offering a pleasant and safe environment for a jog. The diverse landscapes, including pine groves and olive groves, make for an enjoyable outdoor experience for all ages.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Benamejí?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on trails are not detailed, generally, dogs are welcome on outdoor trails in rural areas of Andalusia, provided they are kept under control, especially around livestock, and owners clean up after them. It's always advisable to check local signage for any specific restrictions on individual routes.

Q: Is there parking available near the running routes in Benamejí?
A: Benamejí is geared towards outdoor enthusiasts, and many trailheads or starting points for routes are accessible from the town or nearby villages. While specific parking information for every route isn't provided, it's generally possible to find parking in or near the town center or at designated recreational areas that serve as trail access points.

Q: How accessible are Benamejí's running trails by public transport?
A: Benamejí is a smaller town, and while local public transport options might connect to some nearby villages, direct public transport access to specific trailheads can be limited. Many routes are best accessed by car, allowing for more flexibility to reach various starting points around the municipality.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Benamejí?
A: The running trails in Benamejí are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 5.0 stars from 7 reviews. Over 40 runners have already explored the area using komoot, often praising the varied terrain, scenic river views, and peaceful olive groves.

Q: Are there any challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Yes, Benamejí offers several challenging routes for experienced runners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1287223600/from-encinas-reales-to-benameji-circular-by-the-camino-de-los-olivares" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Encinas Reales to Benamejí — circular by the Camino de los Olivares&lt;/a&gt; is rated as difficult, featuring significant elevation changes and a longer distance, perfect for those seeking a more intense workout.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Benamejí?
A: Given Benamejí's location in Andalusia, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for running, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter days of winter. These seasons provide ideal conditions to enjoy the region's natural beauty.
